info.title: Assortments API
info.description:
The Assortments API allows merchants to manage product assortments at the merchant level in the VTEX Catalog.

>⚠️ This API is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

An assortment is a container that groups collections with explicit inclusion and exclusion rules, enabling granular control over catalog visibility for different business contexts. By combining included and excluded collections, you can enforce two segmentation models:

- **Inclusion model:** All products are restricted by default, and only SKUs that belong to the included collections are visible.
- **Exclusion model:** All products are available by default, and SKUs that belong to the excluded collections are hidden.

This solution is especially useful in B2B scenarios, where each Organization Unit (OU) or contract may require a curated set of products.

## Index

### Assortments

* `POST` [Create product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#post-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment)
* `GET` [Get all product assortments](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#get-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment)
* `GET` [Get product assortment by ID](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#get-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-)
* `PUT` [Update product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#put-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-)
* `DELETE` [Delete product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#delete-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-)

### Assortment collections

* `POST` [Add included collection to product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#post-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-/included-collections/-collectionID-)
* `DELETE` [Remove included collection from product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#delete-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-/included-collections/-collectionID-)
* `POST` [Add excluded collection to product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#post-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-/excluded-collections/-collectionID-)
* `DELETE` [Remove excluded collection from product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#delete-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-/excluded-collections/-collectionID-)

## Common parameters

| **Parameter name** | **Description** | **Type** |
| :---: | :--- | :--- |
| `{{accountName}}` | Name of the VTEX account. Used as part of the URL. | Server variable. |
| `{{environment}}` | Environment to use. Used as part of the URL. The default value is `vtexcommercestable`. | Server variable. |
| `X-VTEX-API-AppKey` | Unique identifier of the [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys). | Authentication header. Must be used together with `X-VTEX-API-AppToken`. Not necessary when using `VtexIdclientAutCookie`. |
| `X-VTEX-API-AppToken` | Secret token of the [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys). | Authentication header. Must be used together with `X-VTEX-API-AppKey`. Not necessary when using `VtexIdclientAutCookie`. |
| `VtexIdclientAutCookie` | [User token](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-user-tokens), valid for 24 hours. | Authentication header. Not necessary when using `X-VTEX-API-AppKey` and `X-VTEX-API-AppToken`. |

accountName.description: Name of the VTEX account. Used as part of the URL.

environment.description: Environment to use. Used as part of the URL.

post.description:
Creates a new product assortment at the merchant level. The `name` field must be unique across the merchant's assortments.

The assortment is created empty, without included or excluded collections, which can later be added through the [Add included collection to product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#post-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-/included-collections/-collectionID-) and [Add excluded collection to product assortment](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/assortments-api#post-/api/catalog/pvt/assortment/-productAssortmentID-/excluded-collections/-collectionID-) endpoints.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

name.description: Assortment's unique name at the merchant level.

get.description:
Retrieves the list of product assortments registered at the merchant level.

Assortments are used to segment the catalog for specific business contexts (for example, B2B contracts or Organization Units) based on the inclusion and exclusion of collection IDs:

- **Inclusion model:** All products are restricted by default, and only SKUs that belong to the included collections are visible.
- **Exclusion model:** All products are available by default, and SKUs that belong to the excluded collections are hidden.

The list returned contains both the included and excluded collections associated with an assortment.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number of the paginated result.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items returned per page.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Cursor indicating the assortment ID after which the next page of results must start. Useful for cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of assortments returned in the response when using cursor-based pagination.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Filters the result to return only assortments that reference the specified collection ID, either as an included or as an excluded collection.

TotalRows.description: Total number of assortments available at the merchant level.

Data.description: List of assortments returned in the current page.

items.description: Object with information about an assortment.

CreatedAt.description: Date and time when the assortment was created, in UTC format.

UpdatedAt.description: Date and time when the assortment was last updated, in UTC format.

IncludedCollections.description: List of collection IDs explicitly allowed by the assortment.

ExcludedCollections.description: List of collection IDs explicitly restricted by the assortment.

Size.description: Number of assortments returned in the current page.

get.description:
Retrieves the details of a product assortment given its unique identifier, including the list of included and excluded collections.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

put.description:
Updates the `name` of an existing product assortment given its unique identifier. The new `name` must be unique across the merchant's assortments.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

name.description: Assortment's new unique name. Must not collide with existing assortments at the merchant level.

delete.description:
Deletes a product assortment given its unique identifier.

>❗ Deleting an assortment does not automatically remove its reference from contracts or Organizational Unit scopes (`assortmentIds`). Make sure to remove those references before or after deletion to avoid orphaned references.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

post.summary: Add included collection to product assortment
post.description:
Associates a collection as an included collection of the specified product assortment. Included collections define the SKUs that must be explicitly allowed when the inclusion model is applied to the catalog segmentation.

The same collection cannot be associated twice with the same assortment. In that case, the request will return a status code `409` error.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

delete.summary: Remove included collection from product assortment
delete.description:

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

post.summary: Add excluded collection to product assortment
post.description:
Associates a collection as an excluded collection of the specified product assortment. Excluded collections define the SKUs that must be hidden when the exclusion model is applied to the catalog segmentation.

The same collection cannot be associated twice with the same assortment. In that case, the request will return a status code `409` error.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

## Permissions

Any user or [API key](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/api-authentication-using-api-keys) must have at least one of the appropriate [License Manager resources](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/license-manager-resources--3q6ztrC8YynQf6rdc6euk3) to be able to successfully run this request. Otherwise, they will receive a status code `403` error. These are the applicable resources for this endpoint:

| **Product** | **Category** | **Resource** |
| --- | ----- | ----- |
| Catalog | Content | **Product and SKU Management** |

There are no applicable [predefined roles](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/predefined-roles--jGDurZKJHvHJS13LnO7Dy) for this resource list. You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

To learn more about machine authentication at VTEX, see [Authentication overview](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/authentication-overview#machine-authentication).

>❗ To prevent integrations from having excessive permissions, consider the [best practices for managing API keys](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/best-practices-api-keys--7b6nD1VMHa49aI5brlOvJm) when assigning License Manager roles to integrations.

>⚠️ This feature is only available for stores using B2B Buyer Portal, which is currently available to select accounts.

You must [create a custom role](https://help.vtex.com/en/tutorial/roles--7HKK5Uau2H6wxE1rH5oRbc#creating-a-role) and add at least one of the resources above in order to use this endpoint.

Content-Type.name: Content-Type
Content-Type.description: Type of the content being sent.

Accept.name: Accept
Accept.description: HTTP Client Negotiation _Accept_ Header. Indicates the types of responses the client can understand.

ProductAssortment.description: Object with information about a product assortment.

CreatedAt.description: Date and time when the assortment was created, in UTC format.

UpdatedAt.description: Date and time when the assortment was last updated, in UTC format.

IncludedCollections.description: List of collection IDs explicitly allowed by the assortment.

ExcludedCollections.description: List of collection IDs explicitly restricted by the assortment.

AssortmentCollectionAssociation.description: Object with information about the association between an assortment and a collection.

collectionId.description: Collection's unique identifier associated with the assortment.

ErrorResponse.description: Object with information about the error.

Message.description: Error message describing the reason the request failed.
